purpose: This repository provides an AI-powered question-answering system for the COS Lending Selling platform. It enables users to query loan data through natural language, translating these questions into SQL queries and generating human-readable responses using AWS Bedrock and Claude LLMs.

apis.purpose: Create or resume a chat session for a user+loan pair
Get all messages for a session with validation of ownership
Submit a user message to the session
SSE stream endpoint that orchestrates the question-answering pipeline

dataEntities.name: Session
dataEntities.description: User conversation session with session_id, username, loan_id and messages

dataEntities.name: Message
dataEntities.description: Chat message with role, content, SQL summary and timestamp

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan entity with loan details, amounts, dates and status

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Actions performed on loans such as transfers or status changes

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Financial transfers related to loans including interest payments and fees

messagingPatterns.name: SSE (Server-Sent Events)
messagingPatterns.description: Real-time streaming of AI responses to the client with token-by-token updates

findings.title: Potential SQL injection risk in SQL generator
findings.description: The SQL generation functionality using LLM responses could potentially contain SQL injection vulnerabilities. Although there are safeguards like regex checks in _ensure_safe(), the system relies on AI-generated SQL which could produce unexpected results. Implement more robust parameterization and additional validation on generated SQL.

findings.title: Session data handling inconsistencies
findings.description: The session manager uses scan operations to find sessions by ID, which will not scale well. The code mentions this could be a concern as volume grows. Implement a GSI on session_id in DynamoDB to avoid full table scans.

findings.title: Credentials caching without proper expiration
findings.description: Database credentials are cached but might not be properly rotated on expiration. The _creds_cache is set to expire after 300 seconds but there's no guarantee that the code properly handles token expiration scenarios. Implement proper credential rotation and expiration handling.
